chunk_summary: The CMAM protocol outlines outpatient care for uncomplicated wasting, including follow-up schedules, escalation criteria, and a multidimensional care package for community pediatric nutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand the outpatient CMAM pathway for managing uncomplicated wasting in children.
Identify follow-up schedules and criteria for escalation in community-based care.
Learn the components of the minimum outpatient care package for malnourished children.
Recognize early warning signs requiring urgent referral or escalation.
key_facts: The CMAM protocol specifies weekly follow-ups for SAM children during the first month, then fortnightly visits.
MAM children require fortnightly follow-ups with monthly weight and height monitoring.
Escalation criteria include fever >39°C, hypothermia <35°C, severe pneumonia, or weight loss for two weeks.
The minimum outpatient package includes nutrient-dense feeding counseling, immunization linkage, and WASH behavior promotion.
Non-recovery after three months or failure to gain weight for two weeks necessitates escalation.
reference_quote: The protocol describes weekly visits for every SAM or severely underweight child during the first month, followed by fortnightly visits; for MAM or moderately underweight children, fortnightly follow-up is used, with weight and height monitored monthly.
document_key_topics: Outpatient CMAM pathways for uncomplicated wasting
Criteria for escalation in community-based care
Components of the minimum outpatient care package
